finding: 4-node repro: {Camera (factoryID=23) + 'Frame B' framing behavior (factoryID=3) + 'Source' layer + 'Transition B' scenenode (factoryID=11)}. All 14 Replicator/Cell tiles were REMOVABLE without losing the bug -> the divergence is NOT the tile wall, it is the FRAMED-CAMERA POSE of a single framed layer (confirms T-H2/S6 resolveFramedWallPose anchor bug). Per-frame engine-vs-FCP: f00-f07 both black (match); f08-f19 FCP=black(0.0) but ENGINE renders B at mean 34.3 (engine brings framed B on-screen ~12 frames too early, at a wrong static pose); f20-f23 FCP reveals B (mean 51->119, the real Frame-B framing ramp) while ENGINE stays FROZEN at 34.3 (mis-posed, static, never matches the reveal). Net: engine paints one static off-centre framed 'Transition B' the whole clip instead of FCP's black-then-framed-reveal schedule. Bug lives in the framing pose/schedule for the 'Frame B' behavior (factoryID=3) + Camera, i.e. evaluator/framing.ts resolveFramedWallPose anchor (bottom-edge [2399,-2145] instead of content centroid) AND its reveal timing, not the replicator grid.
gate_result: FIX-FRAMING (2026-07-14): resolveFramedPose single-behavior reveal+cover fix. Min-repro engine-vs-FCP 8.14 -> 84.42 dB (f08-f19 now black=99dB matching FCP; f20=12.7, f22=9.8, f23=13.8 reveal-ramp). Full GUI-GT gate `fct regress engine` = 0 regressions / 0 improvements (baseline_engine byte-identical). tsc clean, no-hardcode policy PASS. NOTE: the FULL Video_Wall slug (2 factory-3 behaviors) uses resolveFramedWallPose (unchanged, still 9.10 dB) — this fix corrects the SINGLE-framer reveal path exercised by the minimized repro; the wall-pose anchor/schedule for the full slug remains a separate open item (T-H2/S6).

fix_2026_07_15_M_VIDEOWALL.note_vs_fix_framing: COMPLEMENTARY to FIX-FRAMING above: FIX-FRAMING fixed the SINGLE-behavior resolveFramedPose path (the minimized 1-behavior repro -> 84.42 dB). THIS change fixes the FULL slug's 2-behavior resolveFramedWallPose path (wall anchor + animEnd), taking the full Video_Wall slug 9.10 -> 10.24 dB. Both land together; no overlap (different framing pose functions).
changes: parser/index.ts: framing-scene animEnd = MAX Framing-behavior <timing out> (Frame B 1.9686s), not the normalized 0..1 stroke keyframe (was 1.0, truncated the dolly)
evaluator/index.ts: computeWallCenter (bbox centre of the LARGEST replicator) + pass as wallCenter to resolveFramedWallPose
framing.ts: wallCenter param anchors the wall pose on the tile-wall centre instead of the ray-cast corner (2399,-2145)
fix_2026_07_15_M_VIDEOWALL.gate_result: 0 regressions vs baseline_engine (tol 0.3dB); tsc clean; no-hardcode green; baseline re-frozen
fix_2026_07_15_M_VIDEOWALL.remaining: The wall dolly still frames ~1 tile through mid/tail (GT reveals the full 3x3 grid); far-dolly distance + proxy->content reveal SCHEDULE (per FIX-FRAMING's decode: black f00-f19 then ramp) remain for the 2-behavior wall path.

decode_2026_07_15_wrap_freeze.root_cause_correction: DEEPER than the recorded finding (which blamed a mis-posed framing pose). The engine's timeMap.remap() COLLAPSES ALL scene times to 0 for Video_Wall: buildTimeMap sets wrapSec=0.367s because Transition A (drop zone, retimingExtrapolation=1 'wrap') times out at 0.367s, and the retime-wrap loops the playhead back to frame-0 past that. But the Video_Wall transition is authored by the CAMERA DOLLY (two Framing behaviors running to animationEnd=1.969s), NOT the drop-zone crossfade. So every rendered frame evaluates at t=0 -> one static pose. Frozen-at-0 coincidentally scores ~10.24 (holds GT near-tile f0 look); unfreezing (cancel wrap for framing scenes) drops to ~9.0 because the framing pose + wall placement are ALSO wrong.
decode_2026_07_15_wrap_freeze.decoded_schedule: GT near->far->near dolly: f0=one tile (photo A near); f4-f16=dolly OUT revealing full ~3x3+ tile grid (tiles crossfade A->B); f20-f23=dolly IN to one tile (photo B near). framer window [0,0.9676] pulls OUT, Frame B window [0.8676,1.9686] pulls IN. Peak-far f~11.6.
decode_2026_07_15_wrap_freeze.decoded_geometry: AOV=45. content tile 1920x1080 scale1.01 -> near cover ~1317, near fit ~2341. Framer PROXY small shape (half 186.8x254.5) at world Z=5711; framePose(proxy) eye ~Z5109 in front of wall (Z~0), where wall half-width ~4100 fills 45deg frame (35.7 vs 36.4 horiz half-AOV) => full-grid reveal. farDist should be proxy-eye dist ~5109, NOT single-tile fit ~2341 (current code barely dollies).
three_parts_needed_together: 1. timemap.ts: cancel drop-zone wrap when scene has Framing behaviors (factory 3). hasFramingCamera() helper. Fires only Video_Wall (0.367<<end) + Clone_Spin (1.869~=end, negligible).
2. framing.ts resolveFramedWallPose: far=proxy framePose eye dist from wall anchor; near=content cover; schedule=near->far->near triangle over the two windows.

3. WALL PLACEMENT (real blocker): 14 replicators must populate a DENSE grid filling the frame at far. Engine renders ~5 sparse tiles on black (means ~30-50 vs GT ~90-130). Pin-1 has 3x3(9) at origin; 13 others carry 1-3 edge/fill tiles at (2000-6000). Verify all instances render + resolve A/B cell content + land on-screen through the oblique camera.
4. camera orientation: proxy fwd=(0.069,0.422,0.904) tilts view (real - GT tiles show 3D perspective). Keep wall centered/on-screen (tiles slid off in WIP).
decode_2026_07_15_wrap_freeze.status: All 4 must land TOGETHER to net-improve; each partial regresses because frozen-at-0 accidentally scores 10.24. Parts 1+2 verified correct in isolation. Part 3 (dense wall placement) is the remaining blocker. Reverted this tick per Rule 2d; decode preserved for the multi-tick build.

decode_2026_07_15_timing_gate.correction_to_part3: The '~5 sparse tiles / dense wall placement blocker' framing in decode_2026_07_15_wrap_freeze part 3 is REFINED: the 14 replicators are each TIME-GATED with a STAGGERED timing window [in,out] (measured, in seconds): #1 Pin2c[0.63,1.13], #2[0.63,1.13], #3[0.50,1.84], #4[0.03,0.77], #5[0.00,0.53], #6[0.40,0.97], #7 Pin2[0.00,0.37], #8[0.90,1.77], #9[0.50,1.77], #10[0.73,1.77], #11[0.60,1.77], #12[0.60,1.87], #13[0.53,1.87], #14 Pin1[0.00,1.10]. At any instant only the replicators whose window contains t are visible (op=1); the rest are op=0. This is the INTENDED staggered wall build-up (tiles pop in over time), NOT a placement/density bug. At t=0.5 (mid), 4 replicators are live (Pin1 9inst + 3 others 4inst = 13 instances); combined with the correct far dolly ~18/32 project on-screen. So part 3 is really: (a) the wrap-cancel (part 1) so t advances and the staggered windows actually fire, (b) the correct near->far->near dolly + wall-centered anchor (parts 2+4) so the live tiles land on-screen. The tiles crossfade A->B via their Pin1(=A)/Pin2(=B) cell sources (resolveCellImage already maps Pin1->imageA, Pin2->imageB). NET: the subsystem is wrap-cancel + 3-key anchor dolly + correct wall centroid; the replicator content/timing is already parsed correctly. The remaining risk is purely the CAMERA PATH (anchor + dolly schedule), not tile generation.

build_attempt_2026_07_15_3key.result: NET-NEGATIVE, reverted per Rule 2d (gate: 1 reg Video_Wall 10.24->9.52, 0 improvements, all 64 others byte-identical). WIP saved: docs/notes/wip/vw_{framing,index,timemap}_3key.ts.txt.
what_worked: timemap wrap-cancel for framing scenes (hasFramingCamera): time now ADVANCES (was frozen at t=0) — objectively correct, the staggered replicator windows now fire.

3-key anchor camera path in resolveFramedWallPose (key0=near-A static-cam pose, key1=far proxy-eye depth @ wall centroid, key2=near-B content framePose): the NEAR-B END NOW SETTLES ON PHOTO B correctly (GT f23 match, was black/wrong before). Per-tile A/B also renders (Pin1->A sepia, Pin2->B blue mix visible mid).
computeWallCenter changed to all-instance centroid: mid tiles better centered (8.9->9.46).
remaining_gap: MID-WALL still too SPARSE + tiles too small vs GT's full tight grid. Root: at t=0.5 only 13/32 instances live (correct staggered timing) but they span a HUGE ±5000 area (Pin-1 3x3 alone is 8200x2400 wide) so each tile projects tiny; GT shows a COMPACT grid filling the frame. Either (a) FCP frames a TIGHTER region (just the Pin-1 3x3 center, not the scattered decorations at 2000-6000), or (b) the far dolly distance is too large. farDist=proxyEyeDist~5109; wall-fit(Pin-1 halfW4100)=~5496 similar. The scattered replicators at 2000-6000 may be OFF-screen decorations FCP doesn't frame.

f0 near-A renders FLAT SEPIA (solid color, no photo detail) — the near-A key frames staticCamPos XY (2051,-2390) at nearDist but the A tile there shows one flat color; needs investigation (is the standalone Transition A tile rendering its photo, or a placeholder, under the framed camera?).

NEXT: (1) try framing ONLY the Pin-1 3x3 grid (main wall) — exclude the scattered 2000-6000 replicators from the wall centroid + use Pin-1 extent for farDist; (2) debug f0 flat-sepia (A-tile content under framed cam).
